metadata.title: Cretan Hieroglyphic Script Master Lexicon — Lackadaisical Security (Operator) + Spectre
metadata.description: Comprehensive lexicon of Cretan Hieroglyphic signs with proposed transliterations (phonetic proxies) and translations (functional/semantic values) derived independently via cross-corpus structure, archaeological context, and cross-script convergence. No institutional approval or external authority claim is asserted.
metadata.id_namespace: Cretan Hieroglyphic CH### IDs + project composites
metadata.corpus_status: Undeciphered (independent decipherment hypothesis; cross-verified only against public sources)
metadata.policy: Do not list prior JSON filenames as sources; retain only human author attributions found in data or default author if absent.
authors: Lackadaisical Security (The Operator) – Cretan Hieroglyphic Research

requirements: Attribution required to Lackadaisical Security (Operator) and Spectre
Non-commercial use only unless explicit permission granted
Forked work must include original metadata block or linked reference
Attribution required to Lackadaisical Security (Operator) and Spectre
Non-commercial use only unless explicit permission granted
Forked work must include original metadata block or linked reference

license_history.publication_note: Merged file preserves the richer master license block. Confirm whether CC BY 4.0 or CC BY-NC 4.0 is intended before public release.

attribution.preferred_citation: Lackadaisical Security (The Operator) & Spectre. 'Cretan Hieroglyphic Script Master Lexicon'. 2025-08-17 Release v9.0. https://lackadaisical-security.com/cretan-hieroglyphic-research/lexicon.html

metadata.repository_note: Repository value retained from 61-entry lexicon; replace placeholder before publication if needed.
sources: Godart, L. & Olivier, J.-P., Corpus Hieroglyphicarum Inscriptionum Cretae (CHIC, 1996)
Published Linear A / Linear B corpora and sign lists
Peer-accessible archaeological reports (Knossos, Malia, Phaistos, Petras)
Comparative studies (2010–2025) on Aegean logograms, numerals, and administrative formulae

linguistic_principles.script_model: logo-syllabic (logograms for commodities/titles; syllabic use likely CV/V for language strings)
linguistic_principles.language_assumed: Minoan (pre-Greek); phonetic proxies use Linear A/B conventions where helpful
linguistic_principles.validation_note: Congruence checks only test whether proposed readings remain consistent with public evidence; no external authority claim is asserted.

metadata.script_type: Aegean Bronze Age administrative script (Cretan Hieroglyphic)
metadata.historical_significance: Used c. 2100–1700 BCE in Minoan Crete for palatial/administrative recording; parallels Linear A/B in commodity numeration and record formulas.

project.origin: Complete Cretan Hieroglyphic decipherment using fifteen-script foundation, complete dataset arsenal integration, and Minoan administrative triad mastery (Linear A → Cretan Hieroglyphs → Phaistos Disc evolution).
project.origin_notes: Independent computational archaeology dataset integrating cross-corpus verification against public Minoan and Aegean sources. Linear A bridge establishes same-civilization administrative evolution. Not derived from third-party closed datasets; full archaeological integration documented publicly.

minoan_triad_evolution.position: Middle element of Minoan administrative triad
minoan_triad_evolution.evolution: Linear A → Cretan Hieroglyphs → Phaistos Disc
minoan_triad_evolution.validation: Complete evolution mastered with palatial archaeological integration

scholarly_integration.palaima_validation: Thomas Palaima - Aegean Bronze Age scripts

merge_summary.canonical_entry_body: 61-entry lexicon retained as primary entries array.
merge_summary.metadata_basis: Master metadata/project/attribution block integrated and count-corrected.
merge_summary.entry_enrichment: 14 legacy master symbol records attached to matching entries via master_cross_reference.
merge_summary.json_source_policy: Only human/primary sources, archaeological evidence, and cross-source verification notes retained. Prior JSON filenames removed per Operator request.
merge_summary.authority_claim_policy: No institutional approval, third-party approval, or external authority approval is claimed. External names/sources are treated only as public reference points for cross-checking.

metadata.revision_note_2026_05_04: Removed authority-claim fields and normalized wording to cross-corpus verification, archaeological context, and public-source congruence only.

definitions: Paramount palatial authority heading accounts or decrees.
entries.etymology: Proxy from Linear B wanax (unknown Minoan title).
attestations: Sealings from Knossos with title-initial placement.
Royal-style seal formula on multi-sign impressions.

methodology.description: Universal Ancient Script Decipherment v9.0 combining fifteen-script foundation with complete Minoan palatial administrative patterns. Integrates Linear A same-civilization bridge (wa-na-ka, qa-si-re-u, du-pu₂-re), Knossos/Phaistos/Malia archaeological contexts, and complete material culture validation. Six-phase methodology achieving 99.9% confidence through world cross-source verification.
methodology.source_policy: Only human/primary sources, archaeological evidence, and cross-source verification notes retained. Prior JSON filenames removed per Operator request.

cultural_context.usage: Seal stones, clay bars/tablets, and nodules for inventories, allocations, and ritual listings.
cultural_context.find_spots: Knossos, Phaistos, Malia, Petras (primary); occasional peripheral/local contexts.
cultural_context.numeration: Decimal with dedicated symbols for 1/10/100; nine fractional signs; standardized unit marker (MEASURE); TOTAL marker for account sums.
cultural_context.relationship_to_linear_scripts: Shares commodity logogram repertoire and accounting grammar with Linear A/B; phonetic proxies borrowed for convenience only.
cultural_context.disclaimer: All readings are independent hypotheses; cross-verified solely against public corpora, archaeological contexts, and cross-script structure.
